questions.questionText:
A histogram uses frequency density on the vertical axis.
The class 20 ≤ t < 30 has a frequency density of 3.5 and a class width of 10.
The class 30 ≤ t < 50 has a frequency density of 2 and a class width of 20.

(a) Calculate the frequency for the class 20 ≤ t < 30.
(b) Calculate the frequency for the class 30 ≤ t < 50.
questions.explanation: Frequency = frequency density × class width. Area of bar = frequency.

questions.context:
The table shows the lengths (cm) of 80 leaves collected from a tree.

| Length (cm)   | Frequency |
|---------------|-----------|
| 0 < l ≤ 5    | 10        |
| 5 < l ≤ 10   | 24        |
| 10 < l ≤ 15  | 28        |
| 15 < l ≤ 25  | 12        |
| 25 < l ≤ 35  | 6         |

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Calculate the frequency density for each class.

markScheme.desc: Frequency density = frequency ÷ class width used for at least one

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Show that the total number of leaves is 80.

markScheme.desc: Calculates frequency for the two wider classes: 1.2×10=12 and 0.6×10=6

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Calculate an estimate of the mean leaf length.

parts.label: d
parts.questionText: Explain why a histogram with frequency density on the vertical axis, rather than frequency, is more appropriate when class widths are unequal.
parts.correctAnswer: When class widths are unequal, frequency density ensures the area of each bar represents frequency, so bars with wider classes are not misleadingly tall

markScheme.desc: Area of bar = frequency / bars would be misleading if frequency were used with unequal class widths oe
Wider class bars would appear to have more data / frequency density adjusts for class width oe

questions.questionText:
A histogram shows the waiting times (minutes) at a clinic for 60 patients.

| Waiting time (min) | Freq. density |
|--------------------|---------------|
| 0 < t ≤ 4         | 3             |
| 4 < t ≤ 8         | 5             |
| 8 < t ≤ 12        | 4.5           |
| 12 < t ≤ 20       | 1.25          |

(a) Show that the total number of patients is 60.
(b) Find the probability that a randomly chosen patient waited more than 8 minutes.
questions.explanation: Frequency = frequency density × class width. P = favourable / total.
